description: You are a clinical pharmacologist analyzing the therapeutic window of a new oral drug. Using GeoGebra, model the concentration-time profile for a single oral dose using the Bateman equation: C(t) = (D * ka / (V * (ka - ke))) * (e^(-ke * t) - e^(-ka * t)). Use the parameters: Dose (D) = 500 mg, Volume of distribution (V) = 40 L, Absorption rate (ka) = 1.2 / hr, and Elimination rate (ke) = 0.25 / hr. The model must include the therapeutic window boundaries at 5.0 mg/L and 12.0 mg/L. Calculate the 'Time in Therapeutic Range' (duration) where the concentration is above the minimum effective level and save the project as 'pk_analysis.ggb' to ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
